qa_pairs.question: How does a user gain initial access to the Trillium cluster and its associated services like HPSS?
qa_pairs.answer: Users must opt-in for Trillium on the CCDB webpage at [https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/me/access_systems](https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/me/access_systems), which grants access to both the CPU and GPU subclusters. A separate opt-in is available on the same page for HPSS, the nearline system for Trillium. Users with prior Niagara/Mist access (or HPSS through Niagara) before August 5, 2025, were automatically opted-in.

qa_pairs.question: What are the terminal commands for logging into the CPU and GPU subclusters of the Trillium cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To log in to the Trillium CPU subcluster, use `ssh USERNAME@trillium.alliancecan.ca`. For the Trillium GPU subcluster, use `ssh USERNAME@trillium-gpu.alliancecan.ca`. Both require the use of SSH keys and Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) enabled on your CCDB account.

qa_pairs.question: What command should be executed after a user's initial login to the Trillium cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: After logging in for the first time, users should run the command `$ trisetup`. This command populates essential initialization files (.bashrc, .bash_profile, .chsrc, .Xauthority) and creates important directories (.licenses, .local, .ssh, and links to scratch and project directories) in the user's home directory.
